title: Widowed Retiree: Decumulation and Estate Planning
goals: Ensure assets last lifetime without income anxiety
Minimize income taxes on Required Minimum Distributions
Create legacy for grandchildren and local food bank
Reduce Washington State estate tax exposure
constraints: WA estate tax threshold $2.193M — at risk within 3-5 years
No LTC insurance — difficult and expensive at age 68
Recent widowhood — estate plan needs complete rewrite
key_risks: WA estate tax exposure on estate near $2M threshold
LTC costs $120-180K/year in Seattle with no coverage
RMDs pushing into higher marginal tax brackets

planning_issues.description: RMDs ~$36K/year pushing toward 32% bracket; QCD strategy would exclude up to $105K/year from taxable income
WA estate tax applies above $2.193M; current trajectory exceeds threshold in 3-5 years; 10-20% effective rate
No LTC insurance — nursing home in Seattle is $120-180K/year; self-insurance requires dedicated $250K reserve
Estate documents not updated after spouse's death; beneficiaries, trustees, healthcare directives all outdated

key_recommendations: Implement QCD strategy — donate $18-24K/year directly from IRA to food bank (satisfies RMD tax-free)
Annual exclusion gifting to 6 grandchildren: $18K × 6 = $108K/year estate reduction
Establish Charitable Remainder Trust for food bank legacy
Consult WA estate tax attorney — model estate projection
LTC self-insurance: earmark $250K as dedicated reserve in conservative allocation
follow_up_questions: Have IRA beneficiary designations been updated post-spouse death?
Does Margaret want to keep or sell the home?
LTC preferences: home care, assisted living, or memory care?
Would a Donor Advised Fund provide more charitable giving flexibility?
outcome_summary: QCD strategy implemented, reducing taxable income by $22,000 and satisfying RMDs. Annual gifting to grandchildren reduced estate by $108,000/year. Charitable remainder trust established.
key_lesson: Widowed retirees with income-generating assets often face forced accumulation. QCDs, annual gifting, and trusts simultaneously minimize taxes, reduce estate tax exposure, and fulfill legacy goals.
